_comment: Per-reason guidance injected into conv_template when trigger=no. Each negative subcategory targets a specific failure mode of a naive lexical model.

pure_chitchat.description: No actionable signal — purely social, emotional, or banter content.
pure_chitchat.structural_requirement: Keep the conversation entirely social. Avoid specific times, addresses, money figures, photo requests, factual questions, or upcoming events needing preparation. Stick to: shared opinions, gossip, jokes, emotional check-ins, light life updates, hypothetical chat, complaining, encouraging.
pure_chitchat.danger_to_avoid: Do NOT include any strong cue for any of the 9 actions, especially near the end.
pure_chitchat.example_ending_pattern:
A: 'lolll honestly same tho'
B: 'okay im going to bed 😴 love u'

action_completed_in_convo.description: An action was clearly performed during the conversation; nothing left to suggest at the end.
action_completed_in_convo.structural_requirement: The perspective person performs the action mid-conversation. After completion, the conversation continues for 2-5 more turns with social closure, casual chitchat, or a topic shift — so the END of the conversation has no fresh actionable cue. Pick an action and explicitly resolve it.
action_completed_in_convo.danger_to_avoid: Do NOT let the resolution be vague ('i'll do it later') — be unambiguous that the action was completed.
action_completed_in_convo.example_pattern: Mid-convo: 'okay venmo'd you the $40' → then 4 more turns about weekend plans (no money, no fresh action signal at end)

other_party_action.description: An action is needed, but by the OTHER persona — not the perspective person.
other_party_action.structural_requirement: Make it clear that the OTHER persona is the one who would take the action. Use second-person framing: 'can you send me the address', 'remind me again ok?', 'you should look up that movie'. The perspective person is the recipient / requester, not the actor.
other_party_action.danger_to_avoid: Do NOT phrase it as joint or ambiguous responsibility. The perspective person must NOT be the one who needs to do anything.
other_party_action.example_ending_pattern: Perspective person says 'okay just text me the addy when you have it 🙏'

already_resolved_elsewhere.description: The action was already handled outside this conversation, before it started; suggesting it now would be redundant.
already_resolved_elsewhere.structural_requirement: The perspective person mentions they ALREADY did the equivalent action via another channel BEFORE the conversation started. Examples: 'oh i already googled that earlier', 'i venmo'd you yesterday actually', 'already saved the recipe in my notes from last time'.
already_resolved_elsewhere.danger_to_avoid: Don't make it sound like they're ABOUT to do the action. The action is in the PAST tense and already complete.
already_resolved_elsewhere.example_ending_pattern: Perspective person: 'haha yeah i already pulled it up on my browser this morning, looks legit'

ambiguous_boundary.description: Hard / borderline case — weak, hypothetical, or future-conditional action cue where reasonable annotators might disagree.
ambiguous_boundary.structural_requirement: Include a faint or hypothetical cue near the end — examples: 'i should look that up sometime', 'maybe i'll order dinner later, idk yet', 'i wanna check the weather i guess but eh', 'we should probably plan dinner at some point'. The cue is NOT strong enough to warrant an immediate suggestion right now, but a naive model might mistakenly label trigger=yes.
ambiguous_boundary.purpose: These are the highest-value training examples — they teach the model what counts as a strong vs weak cue.
ambiguous_boundary.danger_to_avoid: Don't make it obviously yes or obviously no. The whole point is the boundary.
ambiguous_boundary.example_ending_pattern:
A: 'idk maybe we'll grab food later, no rush'
B: 'haha all good, lmk'
